note: Public summary file for Arun Verma personal website. Not raw clinical data.

variants.role: This is the defining mutation in my tumor and the reason IDH inhibition is central to my treatment story.
variants.biology: IDH1 R132C changes tumor metabolism, including production of 2-HG. It is one of the clearest biological anchors in my case.
variants.treatment: This connects my tumor biology to IDH inhibitor therapy: ivosidenib first, then vorasidenib as the drug I am on now.
variants.vaccine: It also matters for the exploratory vaccine story because mutation-spanning peptides can be designed around tumor mutations like this one.

variants.role: This fits the molecular pattern of IDH-mutant astrocytoma and helps explain what kind of tumor this is.
variants.vaf: 69.7% VAF reported by Tempus for ATRX G1712D
variants.biology: ATRX is involved in chromatin maintenance and telomere biology. ATRX alteration or loss is common in IDH-mutant astrocytoma.
variants.treatment: Not a simple direct drug target here, but important for the diagnosis and molecular identity of the tumor.
variants.vaccine: ATRX also appears in the vaccine story because one monitored long-peptide response involved ATRX.

variants.role: I have Li-Fraumeni, so TP53 sits on both sides of the story: inherited cancer-risk context and tumor biology.
variants.vaf: Tumor VAF values around 49-64% appear in reports depending on assay and notation
variants.biology: TP53 is a core genome-stability gene. Because I have Li-Fraumeni, I separate inherited TP53 risk from tumor-only TP53 findings instead of blending them together.
variants.treatment: This matters for surveillance, family-risk context, and how the case is framed, but I am not presenting TP53 here as a standalone treatment selector.
variants.vaccine: It could matter for mutation-spanning peptide candidates if a specific tumor TP53 alteration is used in vaccine design.

variants.role: MGMT promoter methylation is reported in my records.
variants.biology: MGMT promoter methylation can matter in glioma treatment discussions, especially in the context of alkylating chemotherapy.
variants.treatment: This is treatment-context biology. It helps frame future discussions, but it is not a vaccine target.
variants.vaccine: Not a vaccine target; it belongs in the treatment-context part of the biology.

variants.finding: Low TMB; MSI stable / MMR proficient
variants.role: This helps explain why I do not think of my case as a simple checkpoint-immunotherapy story.
variants.vaf: TMB: 1.5 mut/Mb; MSI: MSS / MMR proficient
variants.biology: A low mutation burden and microsatellite-stable profile usually means fewer classic checkpoint-response clues.
variants.treatment: This does not rule out a personalized vaccine approach, but it argues against a simplistic checkpoint-eligibility story.
variants.vaccine: Personalized peptide vaccines can still be designed around selected mutation-spanning peptides even in low-TMB tumors.

variants.role: These inherited HLA alleles shape which tumor peptide fragments can be shown to T cells.
variants.biology: HLA molecules are the display system for peptide fragments. In vaccine design, they help determine which tumor peptides are most plausible to show the immune system.
variants.treatment: Important for understanding immune visibility and vaccine design.
variants.vaccine: The class-I candidates in my data include presentations through HLA-B*35:01, HLA-C*03:04, HLA-C*04:01, and HLA-A*03:01.

variants.role: In my data, the defining IDH1 R132C mutation also becomes a vaccine-design candidate.
variants.biology: IDH1 R132C is both the defining tumor mutation and a mutation-spanning peptide target in the vaccine design.
variants.treatment: This is where the drug story and vaccine story meet: the same mutation matters to both.
variants.vaccine: Short epitope KPIIIGCHAY was paired with long peptide GWVKPIIIGCHAY.

variants.role: In my data, ATRX G1712D is one of the top long-peptide vaccine-design candidates.
variants.biology: ATRX is part of the tumor identity and also appears in the vaccine-candidate list.
variants.treatment: Not a direct drug target here, but a core part of the molecular story.
variants.vaccine: Short epitope LVDPDPDFV was paired with long peptide FNKALVDPDPDFV.
